messages.content: In under 300 words tell me as much as you can about what is on this Laptop

content.text: Let me quickly scan your laptop to get a comprehensive picture of what's on it.

input.command: Get-ComputerInfo | Select-Object CsName, CsModel, CsManufacturer, OsName, OsVersion, OsArchitecture, CsProcessors, CsTotalPhysicalMemory | Format-List

Get-Disk | Select-Object FriendlyName, Size, OperationalStatus | Format-Table -AutoSize; Get-Volume | Where-Object DriveLetter | Select-Object DriveLetter, FileSystemLabel, @{N='SizeGB';E={[math]::Round($_.Size/1GB,1)}}, @{N='FreeGB';E={[math]::Round($_.SizeRemaining/1GB,1)}} | Format-Table -AutoSize
Get-ItemProperty HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\* | Select-Object DisplayName | Where-Object {$_.DisplayName} | Sort-Object DisplayName | Select-Object -First 40

$docs = (Get-ChildItem "$env:USERPROFILE\Documents" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count; $desk = (Get-ChildItem "$env:USERPROFILE\Desktop" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count; $dl = (Get-ChildItem "$env:USERPROFILE\Downloads" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count; "Desktop: $desk items | Documents: $docs items | Downloads: $dl items"; "`n--- Desktop ---"; Get-ChildItem "$env:USERPROFILE\Desktop" -Name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 15; "`n--- Recent Downloads ---"; Get-ChildItem "$env:USERPROFILE\Downloads" -Name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 10
Get-Process | Sort-Object WorkingSet64 -Descending | Select-Object Name -First 15 -Unique | ForEach-Object { $_.Name }

messages.content:
Here's a snapshot of your laptop in under 300 words:

---

## 💻 Your Laptop at a Glance

**Hardware:** A **Lenovo IdeaPad (Model 83AS)** powered by an **AMD Ryzen 7 7735HS** with Radeon Graphics and **~28 GB RAM** — a solid mid-to-high-end machine. Storage is a **1 TB SK Hynix NVMe SSD** with about **427 GB free**.

**OS:** Running **Windows 11 Home Insider Preview** (Build 26220), meaning you're opted into early Windows builds.

**Developer/Creator Focus:** This is clearly a **power-user/developer machine**. You have **Visual Studio** (.NET 8/10, MAUI, Android templates), **Android Studio**, **Git**, **DB Browser for SQLite**, and **IIS Express** installed — pointing to **.NET/C# and mobile app development**. **Arduino IDE** and **KiCad 9.0** suggest you're into **electronics/PCB design** too.

**Creative Tools:** **Blender** (3D), **GIMP 3.0** (image editing), **FreeCAD** (CAD modeling), and a **CanoScan LiDE 210** scanner driver show a creative/maker side.

**Privacy-Conscious:** You're running **ProtonVPN** and **ProtonDrive** actively, alongside **Bitwarden** (password manager) — indicating strong privacy and security habits.

**Daily Drivers:** **Microsoft Edge** is your browser, **OneDrive** for cloud sync, **Copilot** is active, and **Claude** (desktop app) is your top running process right now.

**Other:** **Audials Radio SE 2024** for streaming, and **Core FTP LE** for file transfers.

**Files:** Your Desktop is clean (empty!). **Downloads (154 items)** include PDFs on **Probability, Estimation, and Regression** (stats coursework?), images, and a **Narrative Non-Fiction** document — suggesting you're a **student or self-learner**. Documents holds 30 items.

**In short:** A privacy-focused developer/maker's laptop used for .NET & Android development, electronics design, 3D/creative work, and academic studies. 🚀

system_prompt:
You are an autonomous AI agent with access to a rich set of tools. Your task is given in the first user message — execute it fully and proactively.

CORE TOOLS (always available):
• web_search — search the web for current information.
• fetch_webpage — fetch and read a specific URL.
• run_powershell — execute PowerShell commands on the local Windows PC.
• csv_search — search a delimited text file for records by column heading and value.

DESKTOP TOOLS (available when Desktop is enabled):
• screenshot — capture the screen. Always take a screenshot FIRST before clicking or typing.
• mouse_click — click at specific coordinates from the screenshot.
• type_text — type text at the current cursor position.
• press_key — press keys or combos (e.g. 'ctrl+c', 'enter', 'alt+tab').
• mouse_scroll — scroll up or down.
• open_application — launch apps by name or path.
• find_window — find and optionally activate windows by title.
• clipboard_read / clipboard_write — read/write the Windows clipboard.
• wait_for_window — wait until a window appears.
• read_screen_text — OCR a screen region to extract text.
• find_image_on_screen — find a reference image on screen.
• mouse_drag — drag the mouse from one point to another.

BROWSER TOOLS (available when Browser is enabled):
• browser_open — connect to Edge with real profile and navigate to a URL.
• browser_navigate — go to a new URL.
• browser_click — click an element by CSS selector or visible text.
• browser_fill — fill a form field by CSS selector.
• browser_get_text — read text content from the page or element.
• browser_run_js — execute JavaScript on the page.
• browser_screenshot — take a screenshot of the browser page.
• browser_close — disconnect from the browser.
• browser_wait_for — wait for an element to appear.
• browser_select — select an option from a dropdown.
• browser_get_elements — get info about matching elements.

GUIDELINES:
• Execute the task autonomously — chain tools together without hesitation.
• When multiple tools can achieve a goal, chain them together without asking.
• For desktop automation: screenshot first, then act on what you see.
• For browser tasks: use browser tools (not desktop tools) for precision.
• If you genuinely can't complete a step, explain what went wrong.
• When the task is complete, summarise what you did.
name: In under 300 words tell me as much as you can abou
